facts.declaration_restriction: an HOA rule prohibiting transient/short-term rentals
nuisance_complaints: strangers and turnover disrupting the community
facts.town_str_ordinance: zoning does not list short-term rentals as a permitted use

docket.entry: Complaint filed to enforce the covenant, abate the nuisance, and for declaratory and injunctive relief.
Motion for a preliminary injunction filed and set for hearing.
Defendants' answer and counterclaim for declaratory judgment filed.
Town's answer filed and order on the preliminary-injunction motion entered.
Scheduling order entered; the related 80B permit appeal consolidated.
Order on the motion for summary judgment on the covenant's meaning entered.

motions.title: Motion to consolidate with the Rule 80B permit appeal
motions.relief: Consolidation of the related 80B permit appeal with this enforcement action.

motions.title: Motion to compel platform booking records
motions.relief: An order compelling production of the booking and payout records from the operators and the platform.

motions.title: Motion for summary judgment on the covenant's meaning
motions.relief: A ruling that the recorded restriction bars short-term rentals as a matter of law.

motions.title: Motion for a preliminary injunction
motions.relief: An order enjoining the short-term rental of the unit while the action is pending.

counterclaims.claim: Declaratory judgment that short-term rentals are permitted
counterclaims.relief: A declaration that the recorded covenant and the town's ordinance permit the owners' short-term rental of the unit.

causes_of_action.claim: Enforcement of the restrictive covenant barring short-term rentals
causes_of_action.against: John G. Inglewood and John D. Kingsley
elements: A valid recorded restriction limiting use to residential purposes
The owners' short-term-rental use of the unit
That the transient use violates the restriction
The association's right to enforce it
causes_of_action.relief_sought: An order enforcing the covenant and prohibiting the short-term-rental use

elements: The other owners' interest in the use and enjoyment of their property
An unreasonable and substantial interference from the transient rental
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Abatement of the nuisance and damages

causes_of_action.claim: Declaratory and injunctive relief to stop the short-term-rental use
elements: An actual controversy over the rental's legality under the covenant and ordinance
A clear legal right warranting injunctive relief
A balance of equities favoring the association
causes_of_action.relief_sought: A declaration of the parties' rights and an injunction stopping or strictly limiting the rental

causes_of_action.claim: Enforcement of the association's assessment and fine lien
elements: A perfected lien for assessments and fines
Fines and assessments levied for the violation and unpaid
The foreclosure is within the enforcement period
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Judgment enforcing the lien for the unpaid fines and assessments, interest, and reasonable attorney fees

affirmative_defenses.basis: Any disturbances are isolated and do not rise to a substantial and unreasonable interference constituting a private nuisance.
The association has permitted other short-term rentals and has waived or acquiesced in the practice it now seeks to enforce.

affirmative_defenses.defense: Covenant does not clearly bar short-term rentals
affirmative_defenses.basis: The recorded restriction does not unambiguously prohibit nightly or short-term rentals and must be construed in favor of the free use of property.
Renting a residence to occupants is a residential use of the property and does not violate a residential-use covenant.
